Read the spec and summarized the key pillars: tracking-first design, rich inputs → Markdown output, deviation as first-class, planned vs. actual separation, configurable coach as policy, forward plan repair, data ownership, and ADHD-friendly UX.
how do you imagine the app? high level

Described a high-level vision with three surfaces — a Today screen you live in during workouts, a lightweight Plan/calendar, and Coach/context settings. AI shows up as ghost targets, inline nudges, and a single "what should I do?" affordance, never as a chatbot.
make a wireframe runnable prototype use the multiple skills I added for you: ios-hig-design, mobile-ios-design, liquid-glass-design run it on my computere asap use sonnet agents to do the coding
Noted that only liquid-glass-design is installed; will apply iOS HIG principles directly and use that skill for the visual language, then hand the build to a Sonnet agent.

Prototype is open in the browser — a single-file HTML prototype with CSS Liquid Glass treatment featuring three working tabs: Today (ghosted prescribed vs. solid actual sets with tap-to-log), Plan (repaired timeline with no shame), and Coach (plain-language config with a working Export .md button).

no, build it as an actual ios app -- I have xcode and everything! don't use card containers -- those are for the web and look bad on iphone -- bleed-edge instead. I should be able to tap next or provide very easily feedback on how easy/hard it felt or write a note or whatever. open it on my ios simulator when ready

idb is available — tapping Start to enter the runner.

App is running clean on the simulator. The runner is TikTok-style with one set per full-screen page, full-bleed backgrounds, circuits/supersets as first-class with mini-maps and block hues, coach cues per exercise, and effortless feedback via effort pills, reps stepper, Note, and Skip. Fixed the ghosting issue caused by safeAreaInset shrinking the scroll container.

yeah, polish -- the spacing feels off -- controls are also too far from the top and from the bottom of the screen[Image #3]. Queued all four fixes: rebalanced spacing with controls anchored to bottom safe area, a live countdown timer for timed sets with depleting ring and haptics, a tappable top pill opening a sheet with all steps grouped by block, and larger reps number with ~52pt +/− glass buttons.

instead of a notes button, let me swipe right on the screen to go to a notes screen -- just like on tiktok you can swipe right to go to someone's profile -- same idea. But the notes shouldn't just be a notes, it should be a note but it should run the agent, like maybe I write "this felt kinda weird, it hurt my back a little" and maybe the agent writes back "ok, try with 50% less weight and tell me how it feels" and it would adjust the next set's weight, or maybe the agent decides "ok, let's skip this one for a few sessions and ease back into it in two weeks" and makes notes and whatnot to change the program, etc. the log & next button we can remove since I would just swipe down to the next exercise when I'm done.
BUILD SUCCEEDED. Installing and capturing the new list sheet + Plank timer.

List sheet works well — sections per block, numbered/A1 badges, current step highlighted, tap-to-jump. Batching the big interaction rework (coach swipe, remove Log & Next, remove Note button, effort rating) into one pass since they all touch the control cluster and navigation.
